Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The task is to produce a list of targeted open ended questions to ask an interviewee about their property onboarding process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The questions should Uncover missing details Understand decision criteria Clarify roles and responsibilities Verify timing and sequencing Learn about exception handling NOT request SQL queries or implementation details

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me think through the process and identify gaps ambiguities and areas needing clarification

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Initial contact documentation phase

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: How does the landlord first contact What channels What specific documents are required Does it vary by city jurisdiction What counts as outdated What are the validity periods for documents Who decides if documentation is sufficient Whats the threshold for rejection What happens if the landlord cant provide documents Is there a follow up cadence How long before the request is dropped Is there a standard checklist What happens after documentation is requested does the process pause or proceed partially

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Recording property details

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Who validates the data entered Is there a double check What happens if theres a discrepancy between documents and actual property Who sets the expected rental price range coordinator landlord or both

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Property manager assignment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: How is workload measured balanced Is there a cap How are these factors weighted What if no manager meets all criteria whats the tiebreaker Who makes the assignment decision A supervisor the coordinator an automated system Can landlords request a specific manager or decline one What happens if the assigned manager leaves or becomes unavailable mid process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Introductory meeting

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Is it mandatory In person vs call Whats documented from this meeting Where are special conditions recorded What happens if expectations cant be aligned can the company decline the property

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Photography inspection

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Who selects hires the photographer and inspector employees or contractors Who pays for them company or landlord Are they scheduled simultaneously or in sequence Does inspection need to happen before photos What standards does the inspector use Internal checklist or legal standards What happens if the property fails inspection How many re inspection attempts Who pays for repairs Is there a deadline for repairs before the property is dropped from onboarding What if landlord refuses to fix issues

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Marketing

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Who approves listings before they go live How are pricing models updated Who has authority to set final price What if the landlord disagrees with the recommended price

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Activation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: What does activated mean exactly what systems Is there a final sign off before activation By whom Can a property be activated before inspection passes Seems no but confirm sequencing

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Tenant screening

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: What are the screening criteria Minimum credit score income multiples Is screening first come first served or best qualified How are fair housing considerations handled during selection Who has final say on tenant selection property manager landlord or company policy What if the landlord rejects all applicants How long does screening typically take What happens to rejected applicants are they notified

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lease finalization

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Standard lease template vs custom clauses who approves custom clauses Negotiation authority how much can the property manager discount without approval Who signs the lease landlord or company as agent Deposit handling where is it held

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Quality checks audits

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: When do these happen before or after move in What happens if an audit finds a problem after the tenant has moved in Are audits performed on every property or a sample Who is responsible for fixing issues found

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Exceptions edge cases

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Special insurance requirements who determines this What triggers it Custom lease clauses who reviews approves Legal team What happens if the landlord withdraws mid process What if the property is in a new city where the company has no presence Properties with existing tenants does the process differ How are disputes between landlord and company resolved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Timing SLAs

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: How long does the entire onboarding take on average Are there SLAs for each phase Which steps can happen in parallel What are common bottlenecks

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Communication tracking

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: How is communication tracked across email CRM PM tools which is the system of record How are stakeholders notified of status changes What visibility does the landlord have into the process status

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Roles

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Property coordinator vs property manager vs leasing agent where do responsibilities hand off Can one person hold multiple roles Who is accountable overall for a successful onboarding

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Success metrics

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: How is a successful onboarding measured What happens after move in when is onboarding considered complete

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now let me organize these into a well structured list The format should probably be grouped by theme process stage for clarity with headers Open ended questions not yes no Let me phrase them as open ended How What happens when Can you walk me through Who decides Describe

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should avoid too many questions maybe 25 to 40 well targeted ones grouped logically

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Use headers and numbered lists Keep each question concise
